subject: A felidar — a large six-legged feline beast — caught mid-stride in a prowling pose, rendered as a loose ink-and-wash sketch on warm parchment
description: A large six-legged feline creature is depicted in a loose, gestural ink-and-wash style on a pale parchment-toned background, characteristic of the Zendikar Rising showcase frame. The beast is shown in profile mid-prowl, body lengthened and low, head turned slightly toward the viewer with bared teeth and a snarling open mouth. Its hide is shaded with warm red-orange and rust wash strokes, with darker ink defining the muzzle, claws and additional pair of forelimbs. The composition is minimal — no environment, no background scenery, just the figure floating on cream-colored paper. Brushy linework leaves visible drag marks and splatter, giving the piece a field-sketch or bestiary-illustration feel rather than a finished painted scene.
foreground: six-legged feline beast in prowling stance, snarling, ink-and-wash rendering
background: blank parchment / cream paper, no environment
emotion: aggressive, alert, predatory — snarling and stalking
